2603.29694.title: Exploring the Impact of Skin Color on Skin Lesion Segmentation
2603.29694.paper_summary: Skin cancer, particularly melanoma, remains a major cause of morbidity and mortality, making early detection critical. AI-driven dermatology systems often rely on skin lesion segmentation as a preprocessing step to delineate the lesion from surrounding skin and support downstream analysis. While fairness concerns regarding skin tone have been widely studied for lesion classification, the influence of skin tone on the segmentation stage remains under-quantified and is frequently assessed using coarse, discrete skin tone categories. In this work, we evaluate three strong segmentation architectures (UNet, DeepLabV3 with a ResNet50 backbone, and DINOv2) on two public dermoscopic datasets (HAM10000 and ISIC2017) and introduce a continuous pigment or contrast analysis that treats pixel-wise ITA values as distributions. Using Wasserstein distances between within-image distributions for skin-only, lesion-only, and whole-image regions, we quantify lesion skin contrast and relate it to segmentation performance across multiple metrics. Within the range represented in these datasets, global skin tone metrics (Fitzpatrick grouping or mean ITA) show weak association with segmentation quality. In contrast, low lesion-skin contrast is consistently associated with larger segmentation errors in models, indicating that boundary ambiguity and low contrast are key drivers of failure. These findings suggest that fairness improvements in dermoscopic segmentation should prioritize robust handling of low-contrast lesions, and the distribution-based pigment measures provide a more informative audit signal than discrete skin-tone categories.
2603.29694.paper_summary_zh: 皮膚癌，特別是黑色素瘤，仍然是主要的發病率和死亡率原因，使得早期檢測變得至關重要。<lb/>人工智慧驅動的皮膚科系統通常依賴皮膚病變分割作為預處理步驟，以將病變與周圍皮膚區分開來，並支持後續分析。<lb/>雖然有關皮膚色調的公平性問題已被廣泛研究於病變分類，但皮膚色調對分割階段的影響仍然未被充分量化，且通常使用粗略的離散皮膚色調類別進行評估。<lb/>在本研究中，我們在兩個公共皮膚鏡檢數據集（HAM10000和ISIC2017）上評估了三種強大的分割架構（UNet、DeepLabV3搭配ResNet50骨幹和DINOv2），並引入一種連續的色素或對比度分析，將逐像素的ITA值視為分佈。<lb/>通過使用皮膚區域、病變區域和整幅圖像區域內的分佈之間的Wasserstein距離，我們量化了病變皮膚對比度，並將其與多個指標的分割性能相關聯。<lb/>在這些數據集中所代表的範圍內，全球皮膚色調指標（Fitzpatrick分組或平均ITA）與分割質量之間的關聯性較弱。<lb/>相反，低病變-皮膚對比度始終與模型中的較大分割錯誤相關，這表明邊界模糊和低對比度是失敗的主要驅動因素。<lb/>這些發現表明，皮膚鏡檢分割中的公平性改進應優先考慮穩健處理低對比度病變，而基於分佈的色素測量提供了比離散皮膚色調類別更具信息性的審計信號。
2603.29694.author: Kuniko Paxton et.al.
2603.29694.authors: Kuniko Paxton, Medina Kapo, Amila Akagić, Koorosh Aslansefat, Dhavalkumar Thakker, Yiannis Papadopoulos
